reasoning: The claim places an "aetheric boundary" between the Atlantic and Pacific basins at 40°W-20°W longitude. This 20° range (spanning ~2,200 km at the equator) is so broad that almost any atmospheric transition feature between South America and Africa would fall within it. The International Hydrographic Organization places the Atlantic-Pacific boundary at Cape Horn (67°W) — well outside the claimed range. No specific observable is defined (pressure gradient, wind shear threshold, temperature discontinuity) that could confirm or refute the boundary's existence at this location rather than elsewhere. The dome confidence is LOW, and without a falsifiable metric, this is unfalsifiable.
